questions.question: What type of logic gate has an output that is HIGH only when all its inputs are HIGH?
options: NAND
XOR
AND
OR
NOR

questions.question: In a multi-processor system with shared memory, what is the primary purpose of a cache coherence protocol like MESI?
options: To manage the physical layout of cache blocks on the silicon die.
To facilitate dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) for power saving.
To increase the hit rate of individual processor caches.
To ensure that all processors see a consistent view of memory, even if data is cached locally.
To prioritize I/O requests over CPU memory accesses.

questions.question: Besides IR drop, which other power integrity concern relates to sudden, large current changes causing voltage fluctuations on the power distribution network, potentially affecting circuit operation?
options: Electrostatic Discharge (ESD)
Thermal runaway
Ground bounce / SSN (Simultaneous Switching Noise)
Electromigration (EM)
Process Variation (PVT)

questions.question: In parallel programming and high-performance computing, what does 'data locality' primarily refer to, and why is it crucial for performance?
options: The process of converting virtual addresses to physical addresses.
The physical location of memory modules on the motherboard.
Ensuring that data is encrypted and secure during transfer between memory and CPU.
Keeping frequently accessed data items close to the processing unit (e.g., in cache) to minimize memory access latency.
Storing all data on the main system RAM instead of using disk storage.

questions.question: In digital IC verification, what is 'coverage' primarily used to measure?
options: The extent to which the design specification has been implemented in the RTL code.
The physical area occupied by the integrated circuit on the silicon die.
The amount of power consumed by the design during verification.
The percentage of the design's logic that has been exercised by the test suite.
The number of engineers involved in the verification process.
